source.title: An integrative theory of prefrontal cortex function

confidence_rationale: Canonical integrative theory of PFC as the substrate for active maintenance of task rules/goals that bias processing in posterior cortex. Extensively corroborated by two decades of follow-up work. Maps cleanly onto REE's need for a rule/policy-level substrate that MECH-261 writes into, though the 2001 framing predates the dACC/lateral-PFC dissociations that later refined it.
failure_signatures: If lateral PFC lesions did not selectively impair rule-guided behaviour while sparing stimulus-response behaviour, the 'active rule maintenance' function would be misplaced.
If posterior cortical bias effects could be produced without sustained PFC activity, the top-down biasing mechanism would be redundant.

mapping.source_claim_statement: PFC maintains representations of task-relevant rules, goals, and context over delays and uses these representations to bias processing in sensory, motor, and limbic cortices toward task-appropriate responses.
mapping.ree_translation: Provides the biological substrate for a lateral-PFC-analog module in REE that holds the currently active rule/policy and projects bias signals into action selection. This is the target MECH-261 writes into during internal_planning / external_task modes but must not be overwritten during internal_replay.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The 2001 paper treats PFC as a unified functional unit; subsequent work (Badre & Nee 2018, Rudebeck & Murray 2014, Mansouri 2020) dissociates lateral-PFC rule representation from vmPFC value, OFC credit assignment, and dACC conflict/effort. REE must not import the 2001 unified-PFC framing wholesale.
mapping.source_context: Primate single-unit + human lesion/fMRI review; task rules in delayed-match-to-sample and Wisconsin Card Sort contexts.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality is very high (foundational, extensively replicated). Mapping fidelity is moderate: the paper's 'PFC' needs to be read as 'lateral-PFC specifically' when ported into REE, because later work showed the functional heterogeneity the 2001 framing papered over. Transfer risk is low for the rule-maintenance function itself; higher if REE inherits the unified-PFC treatment uncritically.
